data.body:
[G3 child-coverage — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with THIS child (S2b b73c: Snapshot cache janitor — reclamation under disk pressure)]

VERDICT: EMPTY G3 findings list. The union of the WHOLE eight-story roster (S1 6d56 materialize core, S2 70c7 cache read/populate, S2b b73c THIS janitor, S3 fa49 gate threading, S4 30b3 attested signing, S4b e308 claim-gate coherence/ADR 0002, S5 b7fa CLI/MCP surfaces, S6 1ba5 docs) covers every parent acceptance/success criterion; no uncovered parent criterion is attributable to this pairing.

4-bucket coverage map (parent AC -> covering child/children; THIS child's role called out):

- Parent AC1 (no code-reading gate reads the server working tree in attested mode; all read a faithful snapshot at the pinned SHA) — FULLY covered by S1 (materialize core) + S3 (gate re-rooting). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. THIS child: the janitor evicts cache entries but neither materializes nor reads gates (its Scope: "Out: the cache read/populate path (sibling cache story); materialization (S1)"). Not an absence.

- Parent AC2 (signed verdict binds verified_at_sha via the manifest channel; reproducible; no payload-schema/version change; prior signatures verify) — FULLY covered by S4 atop S1's immutable-SHA basis. THIS child claims nothing here (no signing). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.

- Parent AC3 (local mode reads in-place checkout AND cannot sign; documented back-out) — covered by S3 (in-place read, unsigned flag) + S4 (signing bar) + S6 (docs). Local bypasses the cache entirely, so this janitor never touches it. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.

- Parent AC4 (concurrent gates on same/different SHAs run without git-lock contention or cross-op corruption; the snapshot cache + JANITOR reclaim disk under a defined free-space watermark (S2) WITHOUT disrupting in-flight reads) — FULLY covered, SPLIT cleanly, with THIS child as the PRIMARY/SOLE OWNER of the janitor + reclamation clause: its AC1 (reclaim under a free-space watermark, LRU by touch-on-read mtime NOT atime, skip grace window, off the hot path), AC2 (secondary max-age cold-trim), AC6 (eviction = rename-to-trash THEN rmtree; no in-place recursive delete of a live entry), AC7 (a second process cannot run a GC pass concurrently — exclusive gc/lock; population stays lock-free), AC4 (byte-total stays consistent under concurrent populate-vs-evict — no TOCTOU drift), AC5 (interrupted rename-to-trash straggler re-drained on a later pass), AC3 (startup sweep clears tmp/* and trash/* and reconciles the byte total). "WITHOUT disrupting in-flight reads" = THIS child's rename-to-trash-not-in-place-rmtree (AC6) RIDING the sibling S2's delete-on-last-close reader safety (70c7 AC3) so open fds survive eviction. The single-flight + delete-on-last-close + no-git-lock-contention halves are owned by S2 (70c7) + S1. No double-implementation; no gap. THIS child is the heart of the "reclaim under disk pressure safely" obligation.

- Parent AC5 (private-repo fetch failure surfaces a descriptive, actionable credential error) — FULLY covered by S1 (raises) + S6 (documents). Fetch is S1's coalesced step; this child claims none. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.

- Parent AC6 (portable: no external tar; no hardcoded /tmp; flock+mkdir fallback) — FULLY covered by S1 (pure-Python extraction, gettempdir/mkdtemp, fcntl.flock + atomic-mkdir fallback) + S6 (docs: tmpdir env, EFS/NFS flock caveat). THIS child's cross-process exclusive flock on <cache>/gc/lock RIDES the flock portability seam S1 establishes but does not re-own the tar/tmp/flock-fallback sub-clauses. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.

- Parent AC7 (operational tunables — free-space watermark, default ref, grace/age windows — configurable with documented defaults (S2/S3)) — FULLY covered, SPLIT: THIS child is the OWNER of the watermark + grace + age window tunables (its AC9: "Watermark, grace window, max-age, and reverify period are configurable with documented defaults"); the default ref is owned by S3 (fa49 AC2/its config-precedence AC) / surfaced by S5; docs by S6. Covered by the roster; this child carries its half. Not an absence.

- Parent AC8 (an ADR captures the architecture + rejected alternatives: PID-lease, payload-schema SHA binding) — FULLY covered, SPLIT: THIS child OWNS the cache/janitor-architecture + rejected PID+heartbeat-lease ADR (its AC10: "An ADR records the cache/janitor architecture and the rejected PID+heartbeat lease"; matches the parent ADR-section assignment "authored in S2"); the payload-schema SHA-binding rejected alternative is owned by S4 (30b3, manifest-channel binding) and the claim-gate/plan-review ADR 0002 is owned by S4b (e308). Each rejected-alternative clause has a named owner. Not an absence.

- Parent final AC (all EIGHT enumerated child stories closed; make lint + full test suite green) — STRUCTURAL/meta epic-completion criterion; covered by the existence of all eight children in the roster (this is story S2b of 8) + each child's own green-bar obligation. Not an absence at this child.

Success-criteria narrative ("every code-reading gate verifies a client-specified, immutable, reproducible snapshot ... never the server's mutable checkout — SAFELY UNDER CONCURRENT DISTRIBUTED USE; signed verdicts bind verified_at_sha via the manifest channel; local preserves the single-developer verify-before-push workflow") decomposes onto parent AC1-AC8. The "SAFELY UNDER CONCURRENT DISTRIBUTED USE" clause — specifically reclaiming disk under pressure WITHOUT corrupting concurrent readers, the "hard part"/riskiest piece — is precisely THIS child's core deliverable (its AC1-AC8/AC10), built atop the immutable-SHA content-addressed cache (S2) + delete-on-last-close reader safety. The "signed/reproducible/verified_at_sha" and "unsigned local" clauses are carried by S4/S3. No uncovered obligation introduced by this child's existence.

Named-consumer / delegation cross-checks (covered-by-named-consumer, not absences): this child's Why/Scope explicitly delegates the cache read/populate path to the sibling cache story S2 (70c7 — "Built ON TOP of the cache story"; "Out: the cache read/populate path") and materialization to S1 (6d56 — "Out: ... materialization (S1)"). It CONSUMES S2's touch-on-read mtime (its LRU signal), S2's incrementally-maintained byte total (the value it reconciles), and S2's delete-on-last-close reader safety (what makes rename-to-trash eviction non-disruptive). Each delegated obligation is claimed by a named sibling present in the roster. Covered-by-named-consumer.

Note (NOT a G3 finding): the sibling cache ticket S2 (70c7) still carries a stale title "+ background janitor" and earlier-vintage comments crediting it with janitor/ADR ACs that no longer live in its body; that janitor/reclamation/ADR work has been correctly split out to THIS child (S2b). That is a label/consistency (G4) staleness note on S2, not a G3 union-coverage gap — the reclamation work IS owned by a named child (this one), so the union covers it.

Conclusion: this child cleanly OWNS the janitor/reclamation slice of the parent criteria — sole owner of parent AC4's "cache + janitor reclaim disk under a defined free-space watermark without disrupting in-flight reads" clause, owner of parent AC7's watermark/grace/age tunables, and co-owner (with S4 for the payload-schema rejected alternative) of parent AC8's ADR. Every parent acceptance/success criterion has a named owner in the eight-story roster; none is left uncovered. NO G3 finding for this pairing.
